mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.13% as of Feb 2005 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate consumption and corporate earnings expectations. Sustained month-over-month employment gains over the preceding 3 months predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151,541 thousand as of Feb 2005 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns following periods of rising PAYEMS do not exceed returns following flat/declining PAYEMS by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) measure forward-looking construction demand and household formation expectations. A sharp decline in HOUST below its 12-month moving average signals weakening demand momentum and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, as housing is a leading demand indicator.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average by more than 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,191 thousand units as of Jan 2005
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with potential demand softening
failure_condition: Trigger is activated but SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC during triggered periods is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 2% annualized over 3 months while remaining positive, it signals supply-side weakness that constrains earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rate falls below 2% but remains positive
failure_condition: Trigger is activated but mean 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly measure consumer and construction demand. When both series show month-over-month growth acceleration simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations. This interaction effect produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 526,057 million and HOUST at 1,191 thousand as of Jan-Feb 2005
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and construction sectors
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day returns when both RSAFS and HOUST accelerate simultaneously do not exceed returns when only one accelerates (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) and federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) interact to determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 2.5%, the Fed is running negative real rates, which typically precedes asset price inflation. This interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 256.3 and FEDFUNDS at 2.13% as of Feb 2005
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during negative real rate periods do not exceed positive real rate periods by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens earnings expectations. A threshold crossing of UNRATE above 4.0% predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as forward guidance is revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% as of Feb 2005 remains below threshold
regime_hint: labor market tightening with potential reversal risk
failure_condition: Trigger is activated but SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC during triggered periods is < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. In late-cycle expansions where GDPC1 growth decelerates below 2.5% annualized, equity valuations compress as terminal growth expectations fall. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when growth is slowing in late-cycle.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate falls below 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,023 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2004
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating growth momentum
failure_condition: Trigger is activated but mean 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC during triggered periods is < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
